pitfall: string .find() method absent in stage0 interpreter
symptom: Runtime error: unknown method .find() on string — emitted once per call site, program continues returning 0 so control flow silently takes the not-found branch
workaround: hand-rolled str_find(hay, needle) using hay.substring(i, i+n) == needle loop — see also training/expression_tagger.hexa str_contains
repro: hexa run any .hexa invoking s.find(x) where s is string

pitfall: exec() on head -n 1000 of 1.28GB jsonl materialises ~10MB string; subsequent .split("\n") + per-line substring ops in stage0 exceed 4GB RSS cap and get SIGKILL
symptom: safe_hexa_launchd Killed:9 after ~30-60s, no stack trace
workaround: do the extraction in the exec() pipeline itself (jq -r .text | head -c 5120) and only return a bounded 5KB sample to hexa
reference: project_hexa_speak_test_health + feedback_hexa_struct_list_alias (stage0 string-heavy ops are N²)
repro: exec("head -n 1000 big.jsonl").split("\n") then loop .substring on each
